q: Summation at a postsynaptic neuron refers to which process?
options: The neurotransmitter molecules are added together to form a larger molecule in the cleft
Multiple EPSPs and/or IPSPs from different or repeated presynaptic inputs are integrated; if threshold is reached, an action potential fires
The postsynaptic neuron counts the number of vesicles released and fires only when a set number is reached
Voltage-gated channels sum their conductances to determine resting potential
explanation: Spatial and temporal summation integrate multiple inputs; if the sum of EPSPs minus IPSPs reaches threshold at the axon hillock, an action potential is generated.
